title: Turazza

{{Quote|Welcome, my little meeping friends. Welcome into my humble service; welcome to Loh'khar's little business.|Loh'khar|<ref name="D1C" />}}
The '''Turazza''' were [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]], short, mottled [[Reptile/Legends|reptiles]] that hatched from [[Egg/Legends|eggs]]. They were able to run faster than a [[Human/Legends|Human]] and had comparable strength, but they sometimes had trouble trying to use items designed for taller beings. Turazzas were seen on several [[Planet/Legends|planets]] during the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], although they rarely spoke any [[Language/Legends|language]] but [[Turazza (language)|their own]]. Turazzas were often considered to be unsuitable for certain kinds of job, and were sometimes seen as little more than pets. The Turazza sisters [[Nizzal]], [[Rizzal]] and [[Vizzal]] acted as assistants to the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Merchant/Legends|trader]] [[Loh'khar]], although their abilities were frequently underestimated by his clients. Similarly, while the Turazza known only as [[Redeye (Turazza)|Redeye]]—because of the color of his [[Eye patch/Legends|eye patch]]—was in charge of the security at the clandestine [[Cantina/Legends|pub]] [[Exovar's Emporium]] on the planet [[Neftali/Legends|Neftali]], many of [[Luskin Exovar]]'s acquaintances and customers were surprised by that fact.

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|Tell yer what. I'll trade yer those eggs for 25 credits. Take 'em off yer hands real easy like.|Galliwig|<ref name="D1C" />}}
[[File:FarStar_loading.jpg|thumb|left|275px|Turazza were strong.]]
Turazza were [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Reptile/Legends|reptiles]] covered with mottled<ref name="D1B" /> [[Color/Legends|red]]<ref name="D1A" /> or green scales.<ref name="WH" /> Anthropomorphic in general shape, a Turazza reached a height of 75 [[Centimeter/Legends|centimeters]],<ref name="D1B" /> reaching only the waist of an adult [[Human/Legends|Human]].<ref name="D1A" /> However, they could scamper<ref name="D3B">{{DarkStryder|adventure=Showdown (The Kathol Rift)|book=The Kathol Rift}}</ref> much faster than a Human could run<ref name="D1B" /> and skitter through the [[Limb/Legends|legs]] of Human-sized individuals, dodging obstacles such as tables that would slow larger beings.<ref name="FF">{{JournalCite|6|Finder's Fee}}, republished as {{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/fiction/f20051007/index.html|text=Finder's Fee}}</ref> At the same [[Time/Legends|time]], the small [[Lizard/Legends|lizard]]-like beings had difficulty handling technology that had been designed with Human-sized users in mind, such as [[Swoop/Legends|swoops]],<ref name="D2A">{{DarkStryder|adventure=Wildfire (adventure)|book=The Kathol Outback}}</ref> with their nailless, three-fingered hands. One individual Turazza was strong enough to carry a big box of [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] [[Power capacitor/Legends|power capacitators]].<ref name="D1A" />

The [[Hair/Legends|hairless]]<ref name="D1A" /> head of a Turazza was dominated by one big snout-like beak that they could use to peck, meep<ref name="D1C" /> twitter, snuffle up,<ref name="D1A" /> titter or gobble.<ref name="FF" /> A Turazza could contort the beak to create facial expressions such as a smile.<ref name="D2A" /> The beak had three upper [[Tooth/Legends|incisors]]—two of which protruded even when the beak was closed<ref name="D1B" />—and also hosted a non-forked, green tongue that darted out when the Turazza laughed.<ref name="D2A" /> A newly-hatched Turazza had a short neck and dark [[Eye/Legends|eyes]],<ref name="D1C" /> but as they aged, the eyes turned green, with neither pupil or iris to be seen.<ref name="D1A" />

The Turazza had two [[Gender/Legends|sexes]], male<ref name="WH" /> and female. A Turazza [[Egg/Legends|egg]] was fist-sized and gray-green speckled.<ref name="D1C" /> Three Turazza eggs were considered to have little monetary worth;<ref name="D1B" /> [[Galliwig]], owner of [[Galliwig's Cantina]], estimated that twenty-five [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] was a generous valuation. When hatching, the baby Turazza made the egg stir and shift, and then the baby pecked its way out, carefully breaking the upper part of the egg with the beak and then pushing the pieces of the shell until it could poke out its head.<ref name="D1C" /> After this, the baby bonded with the first mobile individual it could see, with some people believing that the bond could be empathic or even [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathic]].<ref name="D1B" /> Three sisters born from different eggs were similar in appearance, although not physically identical.<ref name="FF" />

==Society and culture==
[[File:Black_Dust_Tavern.jpg|thumb|right|225px|Turazza mixed in the galaxy.]]
{{Quote|A little going away present from Nizzal.|Gorak Khzam throwing a grenade provided by a Turazza|<ref name="D2A" />}}
Turazzas were sentient creatures with individual needs and goals. That was true even when talking about three sisters bonded to the same person; they followed the leader and obeyed his whims, but each of the Turazza had her own skills and her own goals, sometimes in opposition to those of her siblings.<ref name="D1B" /> Nonetheless, Turazza sisters working together were close and, should one of them desert the group, the others spent a mourning period of several days keening loudly, going without [[Sleep/Legends|sleep]] or food, and snuggling up with each other.<ref name="D2A" />

The [[Turazza (language)|Turazza]] [[Language/Legends|language]] included short squeaks, heeps and meeps that sounded savage to members of other species.<ref name="D1B" /> Rarely was a non-Turazza fluent in that language,<ref name="WH" /> nor was every Turazza fluent in [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]].<ref name="D1B" />

==History==
As of [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], the Turazza had already joined the [[galactic community]], having a presence on several [[Planet/Legends|planets]].<ref name="2BBY">''[[Finder's Fee]]'' and ''[[Out of the Cradle]]'' take place in [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]]</ref> Their combat prowess was not valued: during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the possibility of a Turazza being security chief of an underground locale with a high criminal presence was a surprise to many individuals.<ref name="WH" /> As of [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Turazza were perceived more as pets than as sentient beings, even by operatives of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].<ref name="D3B" />

==Turazza in the galaxy==
{{Quote|I don't know… Go ask him.|Luskin Exovar answering the question: How did you end up with a Turazza as security chief|<ref name="WH" />}}
[[File:Redeye.jpg|thumb|left|110px|The Turazza nicknamed "Redeye"]]
The [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Merchant/Legends|free-trader]] [[Loh'khar]] "the Finder" acquired three Turazza eggs as payment for work. He decided to keep the eggs<ref name="D1C" /> and raised the three Turazza sisters, [[Nizzal]], [[Rizzal]] and [[Vizzal]], as his minions to run errands for him.<ref name="D1B" /> As of [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], Loh'khar and his Turazza were active on [[Kelada/Legends|Kelada]], a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]],<ref name="FF" /> working for both the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]].<ref name="D1B" /> That same [[Standard year/Legends|year]], a hunchbacked Turazza was seen in the [[Black Dust Tavern]] in [[Soco-Jarel Spaceport]] on the planet [[Socorro/Legends|Socorro]]. That Turazza was in the locale when the [[Sluissi]] [[Secles Uslopos]] and his [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] underlings entered to try to recruit another patron, [[Drake Paulsen]].<ref name="OC">{{JournalCite|2|Out of the Cradle}}, republished as {{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/fiction/f20040730/index.html|text=Out of the Cradle}}</ref> Also during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], a green-skinned Turazza known as [[Redeye (Turazza)|Redeye]]—due to the red [[Eye patch/Legends|eye patch]] over his right eye—served the Human [[Luskin Exovar]] both as a partner during Exovar's days as a [[Scout/Legends|scout]] and then as the security chief of [[Exovar's Emporium]], a secret, underground [[Hotel/Legends|hotel]]-restaurant on the planet [[Neftali/Legends|Neftali]]. Such employment was a surprise to many who underestimated Redeye because he was a Turazza.<ref name="WH" />

In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Loh'khar and his trio of Turazza sisters joined the crew of the [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|CEC]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' under her Human [[Captain/Legends|Captain]], [[Keleman Ciro]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. The ''FarStar'' was engaged in a [[DarkStryder Campaign|mission]] to chase fugitive Human [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]] through the unexplored [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]], and at the same time create contacts for the New Republic in the area—as well as finding information about the [[DarkStryder technology]] used by Sarne.<ref name="D1A" /> Loh'khar and the ship's [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] security chief, [[Gorak Khzam]], wanted to find the source of Sarne's strange artifacts and sell them on the [[Black market/Legends|black market]], but they chose to remain in the ''FarStar'' as long they could profit from it. The Turazza sisters supported Loh'khar's plan to desert the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] at the opportune moment, and they wanted to take part in it.<ref name="D3A">''[[The Kathol Rift]]''</ref> The Turazzas helped in surveillance tasks whenever the crew went to a planet's surface, enjoying that task.<ref name="D2A" /> They also helped Loh'khar maintain secret communication channels with external allies,<ref name="D3B" /> with Loh'khar's personal goals being their priority.<ref name="D4B">''[[Endgame]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The Turazza first appeared in a [[1994]] illustration by [[Mike Vilardi]] published in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 2|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 2]] to accompany [[Patricia A. Jackson]]'s short story "[[Out of the Cradle]]." The species was named and expanded in [[1995]]'s ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'', written by [[Peter M. Schweighofer]] for [[West End Games]]'s ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. The boxed campaign included new illustrations of Turazza by Vilardi. Author [[Paul Danner]] later included the Turazza "Redeye" in his book ''[[Wretched Hives of Scum & Villainy]]'', also for West End Games.
